title: Repository State, References, and Revision Algebra · Graph Lens: Gita Readonly Forensic Baseline
summary: A release repository uses symbolic refs, detached work, replace refs, ambiguous names, and complex revision sets; a maintainer must answer an audit question without moving any ref. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita readonly forensic baseline increment in repository-state and revision-query proof. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Capture repository boundaries, object format, refs, worktrees, index state, operation state, and recovery anchors before investigation.
Use parent selection, first-parent history, generation, and merge-base evidence without confusing display order with causality.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Readonly Forensic Baseline
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G90 is a theory in a disposable repository. the audit mismatch is confined to commits reachable from exactly one reviewed tip. The learner must predict left and right markers expose exclusive ancestry without moving a reference, run git rev-list --left-right --ancestry-path release...topic -- src/policy.ts, explain two right-only policy commits and one left-only release fix define the review set, test this changed condition: replace topic with topic^{tree} and reject the query because a tree has no commit ancestry, and preserve this recovery boundary: read-only query; retain refs/audit/before-query at the reviewed release tip.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Readonly Forensic Baseline contract for the supplied data.

content.text: Capture repository boundaries, object format, refs, worktrees, index state, operation state, and recovery anchors before investigation. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Gita Readonly Forensic Baseline while producing repository-state and revision-query proof.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G90 is a worked example in a disposable repository. the audit mismatch is confined to commits reachable from exactly one reviewed tip. The learner must predict left and right markers expose exclusive ancestry without moving a reference, run git rev-list --left-right --ancestry-path release...topic -- src/policy.ts, explain two right-only policy commits and one left-only release fix define the review set, test this changed condition: replace topic with topic^{tree} and reject the query because a tree has no commit ancestry, and preserve this recovery boundary: read-only query; retain refs/audit/before-query at the reviewed release tip. Competency probe: Capture repository boundaries, object format, refs, worktrees, index state, operation state, and recovery anchors before investigation. A defensible solution applies Gita Readonly Forensic Baseline, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to repository-state and revision-query proof.

content.code:
# Worked focus: gita-readonly-forensic-baseline — Capture repository boundaries, object format, refs, worktrees, index state, operation state, and recovery anchors before investigation.
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 2
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the audit mismatch is confined to commits reachable from exactly one reviewed tip
git rev-list --left-right --ancestry-path release...topic -- src/policy.ts
# expected: left and right markers expose exclusive ancestry without moving a reference
# observation: two right-only policy commits and one left-only release fix define the review set
# changed-case: replace topic with topic^{tree} and reject the query because a tree has no commit ancestry
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: read-only query; retain refs/audit/before-query at the reviewed release tip
# transfer-limit: verify revision parsing against installed Git help before applying the query to a real repository
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Gita Readonly Forensic Baseline evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-readonly-forensic-baseline-1l0k61" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Readonly Forensic Baseline into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-readonly-forensic-baseline-1l0k61
# scenario-key: trace-1l0k61 probe-16k0l1 boundary-0k611l owner-1l0k61x fault-1l0k61z
# before-ref: main=a11ce1l candidate=b00b161
# hypothesis: the audit mismatch is confined to commits reachable from exactly one reviewed tip
git rev-list --left-right --ancestry-path release...topic -- src/policy.ts
# expected: left and right markers expose exclusive ancestry without moving a reference
# observation: two right-only policy commits and one left-only release fix define the review set
# changed-case: replace topic with topic^{tree} and reject the query because a tree has no commit ancestry
# after-ref: main=a11ce1l candidate=c0ffee1
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: read-only query; retain refs/audit/before-query at the reviewed release tip
# transfer-limit: verify revision parsing against installed Git help before applying the query to a real repository

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Capture repository boundaries, object format, refs, worktrees, index state, operation state, and recovery anchors before investigation.
